genus_note: Monotypic genus Ixoreus — sole living member

plumage.auricular: Dark ear patch connecting to breastband

theory_nodes.concept: Sustained tone as drone / pedal point
examples: Single frequency held for maximum duration — the whole note tied to another whole note
Tanpura drone in Indian classical music — the harmonic reference
Bagpipe drones — continuous tone while melody plays above
Bach Passacaglia in C minor — sustained bass pedal
La Monte Young sustained-tone compositions — drone as primary material
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Pedal point — a held note that serves as harmonic anchor while time passes around it. The drone beneath all other motion.

examples: Carrier tone (2.5-5 kHz) modulated by rapid amplitude fluctuations (20-50 Hz)
AM radio transmission: carrier × modulation signal = complex output with sidebands
Ring modulation in analog synthesizers (Moog, EMS VCS3, ARP 2600)
FM (wren, degree 1) vs. AM (thrush, degree 2) — both fundamental modulation types
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: AM synthesis — amplitude modulation producing timbral complexity from a single carrier tone. The biological AM transmitter.
theory_nodes.cross_reference: AMR (AM Radio research project) — same mathematics, different medium

theory_nodes.concept: Minimal melody — one note at a time
examples: Vocalization stripped to theoretical minimum: one pitch, sustained, then silence, then one different pitch
Webern's pointillism — each note isolated, given maximum weight by surrounding silence
La Monte Young — sustained tones as complete compositions
Brian Eno — Music for Airports (1978) — ambient sustained-tone music
Miles Davis — one note, perfectly placed, allowed to ring
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Reductive melody — fewer notes, more weight per note. The opposite of complexity through density. Simplicity as aesthetic principle.

examples: 3-8 seconds of silence between notes — the pause IS the rhythm
Japanese aesthetic of ma (間) — the interval, the pregnant pause
Negative space in visual art — emptiness defines form
Pre-delay in recording engineering — silence before reverb builds
Degree 0 silence returns here as frame around each tone (same principle, new application)
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Rest as structural rhythm. The silence is not absence — it is the compositional element that gives each note its weight.

examples: 2.5-5 kHz falls at peak human auditory sensitivity (ear canal resonance ~3 kHz)
Descending pitch tendency perceived as melancholy in Western music tradition
AM-produced roughness perceived as darker, more complex, more 'felt'
Sustained duration allows listener to inhabit the frequency — emotional weight through time
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The haunting quality is acoustic physics: peak sensitivity frequency + AM roughness + sustained duration + descending tendency = perceived melancholy.

examples: 2.5-5 kHz optimized for conifer forest propagation (below atmospheric absorption, above trunk scattering)
Sustained note duration resists transient masking (sonar principle — longer ping carries further)
Inter-note silence as pre-delay — allows reverberant tail to decay before next note
Dense canopy + mature conifers = the acoustic environment the song is designed for
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Acoustic architecture — the performance space shapes the music. The forest is the reverb chamber, the concert hall, the studio.

theory_nodes.concept: The voice of the rain forest — cultural acoustic identity
examples: Independently described as 'the most haunting sound in the PNW forest' by multiple sources
Used in nature documentaries and soundscape ecology as PNW acoustic signature
Haskell (2017) cites as defining voice of Pacific Northwest forest ecology
Simplicity more evocative than complexity — one note can do what a hundred cannot
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Acoustic identity — a single sound that defines a place. The melodic signature of the Pacific Northwest, reduced to one note.

cross_references.title: Wings of the Pacific Northwest
cross_references.connection: Thrush taxonomy, Turdidae placement, distributional context; this release adds AM synthesis mapping and sustained-tone theory

cross_references.title: Living Systems of the PNW
cross_references.connection: Mature conifer forest ecology, altitudinal migration, forest-floor foraging ecology

cross_references.title: Bio-Physics Sensing
cross_references.connection: Syringeal AM production, amplitude modulation mechanics, acoustic propagation modeling in conifer forest

cross_references.title: AM Radio
cross_references.connection: Direct parallel: biological AM synthesis = AM radio transmission. Carrier × modulation = complex signal. Same mathematics, different medium.

cross_references.title: Seattle 360 Music
cross_references.connection: Part A parallel: Wayne Horvitz at degree 2 — sustained tone, atmospheric space, avant-jazz/chamber at the boundary of composed and ambient

cross_references.title: Great Blue Heron (Degree 0)
cross_references.connection: Silence at degree 0 returns as the frame around each note. Same principle (structured silence), new application (negative space around sustained tone).

cross_references.title: Pacific Wren (Degree 1)
cross_references.connection: Direct predecessor and polar opposite. FM cascade vs. AM sustain. 100 notes/bout vs. 1 note/bout. Complexity through density vs. complexity through sustained presence.

part_a_parallel.shared_principle: Sustained tone, atmospheric space, the note that hangs in the air. Music at the boundary of composed and ambient. One note is enough when it is the right note.
resonance_points: Sustained atmosphere: Horvitz's compositions create spaces where single notes and chords hang in the air — the thrush's song IS that hanging note in the forest
The composed-ambient boundary: Horvitz works where composition meets improvisation meets environment — the thrush sings where biology meets acoustics meets ecology
Seattle specificity: Horvitz is a Seattle musician; the Varied Thrush is heard singing in Seattle's Capitol Hill neighborhood — both are voices of the same winter city
Fewer notes, more weight: Horvitz's chamber works use space and restraint — the thrush's one-note-at-a-time approach is the ultimate expression of this aesthetic

degree_arc.position: Third vertex of the opening triangle
degree_arc.progression: Silence (0°) → cascade complexity (1°) → single sustained tone (2°)
degree_arc.key_insight: The circle is non-monotonic. Complexity does not increase linearly. Degree 2 is LESS complex than degree 1 in note density but introduces new dimensions (AM synthesis, sustained tone, drone aesthetics) that degree 1 does not contain.

conservation.state_status: Common but habitat-dependent; population tied to mature conifer forest extent
primary_threats: Loss of mature and old-growth conifer forest (breeding habitat)
Climate-driven shifts in montane forest distribution
Forest fragmentation reducing contiguous breeding habitat
Window strikes during winter urban residency
conservation.migration_note: Altitudinal migrant — breeds in montane forest, winters in lowland forest and urban areas with mature conifers

key_sites.name: Capitol Hill residential areas
key_sites.description: Mature conifers and berry-producing shrubs in residential yards; documented winter singing

key_sites.name: Discovery Park
key_sites.description: Mature second-growth forest with dense canopy

key_sites.name: Seward Park
key_sites.description: Old-growth forest remnant within city limits

key_sites.name: Olympic National Park (montane forest)
key_sites.description: Mature conifer forest from 2,000-5,000 ft; high breeding density
